differential_diagnoses: Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal cavernoma
next_steps: CT angiography of the abdomen to evaluate for mesenteric ischemia and detailed portal venous anatomy
Diagnostic paracentesis if ascites develops or for further liver disease characterization
Transjugular liver biopsy with hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG) measurement
Hematology consultation for hypercoagulability workup (including JAK2, PNH, and protein C/S/ATIII)
Consultation with vascular surgery for evaluation of mesenteric ischemia and potential revascularization

differential_diagnoses: Chronic mesenteric ischemia secondary to extensive mesenteric venous thrombosis
Chronic portal vein thrombosis (cavernous transformation) with portal hypertension
Myeloproliferative neoplasm (e.g., JAK2-mutated) presenting as splanchnic vein thrombosis
Hepatic schistosomiasis with secondary portal hypertension
Post-traumatic portal vein injury leading to chronic thrombosis
next_steps: CT angiography of the abdomen to evaluate mesenteric artery patency and splanchnic venous anatomy
Testing for JAK2 V617F mutation and other myeloproliferative disorder markers
Measurement of hepatic venous pressure gradient (HVPG) during transjugular liver biopsy
Stool examination for ova and parasites (Schistosoma) and serology
Consultation with vascular surgery to discuss surgical or endovascular management of mesenteric ischemia
Nutrition assessment to manage sitophobia and weight loss
